message:DataSet.SUGGESTED_CITATION: International Monetary Fund. International Reserves and Foreign Currency Liquidity (IRFCL), https://data.imf.org/en/datasets/IMF.STA:IRFCL. Accessed on [current date].
message:DataSet.METHODOLOGY_NOTES: International Reserves and Foreign Currency Liquidity Data Template Guidelines; Balance of Payments and International Investment Position Manual; Sixth Edition (BPM6). On January 1, 2026, Bulgaria became the 21st country to join the Euro Area, at a fixed conversion rate of 1.95583 BGN = 1 EUR. Data for Bulgaria are included in aggregates for the Euro Area from April 1, 2026, for all periods from January 1, 2026, onwards.
message:DataSet.KEYWORDS_DATASET: International Reserves;Foreign Currency Liquidity;IRFCL;Reserves Data Template;Reserves Template;reserve assets;reserves;official reserves;official foreign currency assets
message:DataSet.FULL_DESCRIPTION: The International Reserves and Foreign Currency Liquidity dataset (Reserves Data Template) includes the country's reserve assets and other foreign currency assets. It also includes scheduled outflows and inflows of those assets, contingent assets/liabilities, and other information on international reserves and foreign currency liquidity statistics by economy. The data present a comprehensive account of official foreign currency assets with scheduled and contingent outflows and inflows of those assets, providing essential inforfmation to measure net internatioanl reserves and assess the external vulnerability of the country. Data are collected from national authorites using the IMF Data Template on International Reserves and Foreign Currency Liquidity.
